description.content: CRUD operations for news content items

Retrieves a paginated list of news items from Sitefinity CMS. Supports OData query options for filtering, sorting, and selecting specific fields. Returns news items that are visible to the authenticated user.

query.description: Maximum number of items to return (OData $top)
Number of items to skip for pagination (OData $skip)
Comma-separated list of fields to return

Creates a new news item in Sitefinity CMS. The request body must contain the required fields for the news content type including title and content. The item is created in draft state by default.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Generic content item operations across all content types
The Sitefinity instance hostname
(Required) The unique GUID identifier of the content item

The Sitefinity CMS Content API provides RESTful access to all content types defined in a Sitefinity instance. It exposes endpoints for creating, reading, updating, deleting, publishing, and unpublishing content items across all dynamic content types (news items, blog posts, events, etc.) and custom types defined through the CMS content model. The API follows OData conventions for filtering, sorting, and pagination. Authentication is handled via forms-based authentication or token-based auth depending on the Sitefinity configuration.
